body:
**AI-generated review based on @D3Hunter's standards; manual review will be performed after all comments are resolved.**

### Summary

- Total findings: 2
- Inline comments: 2
- Summary-only findings (no inline anchor): 0

<details>
<summary>Findings (highest risk first)</summary>

#### ⚠️ **[Major]** (1)

1. CleanupAllLocalEngines no longer tells callers that failures are log-only (`pkg/lightning/backend/local/local.go:839`)

#### 🟡 **[Minor]** (1)

1. Local-sort retry cleanup change still has no focused regression coverage (`pkg/dxf/importinto/task_executor.go:212; pkg/lightning/backend/local/engine_mgr.go:480; local-sort subtask error handling`)

</details>

comments.body:
#### ⚠️ **[Major]** CleanupAllLocalEngines no longer tells callers that failures are log-only

**Why**
The exported cleanup API changed from an `error`-returning call to a fire-and-forget method, but the public name and doc comment still read like a normal cleanup operation instead of a best-effort, internal-logging path.

**Scope**
pkg/lightning/backend/local/local.go:839

**Risk if unchanged**
Future callers can reasonably assume cleanup success is guaranteed or observable at the call site, which makes failure handling and operational debugging depend on hidden logger behavior instead of an explicit contract.

**Evidence**
In `pkg/dxf/importinto/task_executor.go`, the old `cleanupErr` handling was removed and the call site now just invokes `CleanupAllLocalEngines(...)`. At the same time, `pkg/lightning/backend/local/local.go` changed `CleanupAllLocalEngines` to return no value, while its unchanged doc comment still only says it is 'used for best-effort cleanup on error' and does not say that errors are swallowed and only emitted through internal logging.

**Change request**
Prefer a name or doc update that makes the new contract explicit, for example documenting that this is a best-effort cleanup helper whose failures are logged internally and never returned to the caller.

#### 🟡 **[Minor]** Local-sort retry cleanup change still has no focused regression coverage

**Why**
This patch changes the subtask failure cleanup sequence for local sort and turns `CleanupAllLocalEngines` into a fire-and-forget path, but the changed retry/cleanup behavior is still untested. For a partial-failure path that is meant to keep retries and restarts safe, the current evidence is only static reasoning.

**Scope**
pkg/dxf/importinto/task_executor.go:212; pkg/lightning/backend/local/engine_mgr.go:480; local-sort subtask error handling

**Risk if unchanged**
A later refactor can easily break the error cleanup ordering or the best-effort cleanup invocation without CI catching it, leaving stale local engines behind after a failed import and making retry/restart behavior less predictable for operators.

**Evidence**
The diff changes only production code in `pkg/dxf/importinto/task_executor.go`, `pkg/lightning/backend/local/engine_mgr.go`, and `pkg/lightning/backend/local/local.go`. A repository search under `pkg/**/*_test.go` finds no test referencing `CleanupAllLocalEngines`, and no import-into test exercises the local-sort `RunSubtask` failure branch that now defers cleanup before opening engines. Make sure retry/cancel path is covered.

**Change request**
Add UT for it: cover a local-sort `RunSubtask` failure after engines are opened, and assert the cleanup path runs deterministically on error. Please add at least one negative case that verifies retries start from a cleaned engine state, plus a case where cleanup itself reports an error so the logging-only behavior remains intentional.
